stubInfo.title: Stub Mechanism Overview
stubInfo.body1: During the programming process, sftool downloads a stub program to the device RAM. The host computer then interacts with this stub to execute flash read, erase, and write operations.
stubInfo.body2: The stub includes a limited set of built-in flash drivers. If the flash memory being used is not supported by these built-in drivers, an external flash driver must be manually injected to ensure proper operation.
stubInfo.body3: Modifying these configurations is recommended only when the flash memory model and its specific parameters are fully understood.
stubInfo.body4: For initial use or uncertain scenarios, it is recommended to prioritize the built-in flash driver configurations.

deviceConnection.chipSearchPlaceholder: Select or search for a device part number

deviceConnection.deviceRemovedNotice: Port {port} was removed. The connection has been disconnected automatically. Reconnect the device and connect again manually.
deviceConnection.deviceRecoveredNotice: Port {port} is available again. The device selection has been restored. Connect again manually when ready.
deviceConnection.deviceRemovedLog: Detected that port {port} was removed and disconnected the current session automatically
deviceConnection.deviceRecoveredLog: Detected that port {port} is available again. You can reconnect manually now.

stubConfig.title: Stub Configuration
stubConfig.invalidConfig: The configuration contains errors and cannot be enabled.
stubConfig.configHasErrors: The configuration contains errors. Please check and correct them on the Stub configuration page.
stubConfig.externalStubMissing: The selected external stub file is unavailable. Re-select it on the Stub Configuration page.
stubConfig.externalStubUnavailable: The external stub file is unavailable. Check the path or choose it again.

messages.embeddedOnly: The connection will use the embedded stub directly.
messages.configOnly: The connection will use the embedded stub and then apply the custom Stub Configuration JSON.
messages.externalOnly: The connection will use the selected external stub file directly.
messages.externalWithConfig: The connection will load the selected external stub file first, then apply the custom Stub Configuration JSON.
messages.externalMissing: External stub is still enabled, but the selected file cannot be accessed now. Connection will fail until the path is fixed.
